leagueBriefing: The NHL's milestone 70,000th game night saw the Los Angeles Kings reclaim a playoff spot while the St. Louis Blues kept their slim postseason hopes alive with a dramatic comeback win.

games.headline: Kings reclaim playoff spot as Kopitar nears franchise history
games.brief: The Los Angeles Kings surged into a first-period lead and weathered a late push from the Islanders to secure a vital two points in the Western Conference playoff race. Captain Anze Kopitar scored his eighth of the season, moving him just one point shy of tying Marcel Dionne's legendary franchise scoring record. While New York's Emil Heineman netted twice to make it a contest, the Kings' early three-goal barrage proved too much to overcome, lifting Los Angeles back into a wild-card position.
bullets: Anze Kopitar now sits at 1,306 career points, just one behind Marcel Dionne for the most in Kings history.
Adrian Kempe recorded his team-leading 26th goal of the season with a highlight-reel one-handed deflection.
Trevor Moore opened the scoring just 3:33 into the game with a solo breakaway effort after blocking a shot.
The victory propelled the Kings past Seattle and San Jose into the second wild-card spot in the West.
Emil Heineman scored both goals for the Islanders, marking his 17th and 18th tallies of the year.
games.context: The win moves the Kings back into the Western Conference playoff picture for the first time since early February.

highlights.title: Kings vs. Islanders | NHL Highlights | March 13, 2026

games.headline: Robert Thomas caps stunning Blues comeback with last-second overtime winner
games.brief: The St. Louis Blues pulled off a miracle at Enterprise Center, erasing a two-goal deficit late in the third period before Robert Thomas sealed the victory with just nine seconds remaining in overtime. Edmonton appeared to have the game in hand following goals from Kasperi Kapanen and Connor McDavid, but the Blues' resilience shone through with tallies from Pius Suter and Cam Fowler in the final eight minutes of regulation. The win keeps St. Louis within striking distance of the wild-card hunt while sending the Oilers home with a frustrating end to their road trip.
bullets: Robert Thomas scored the game-winner with only 9.1 seconds left in overtime, his 16th goal of the season.
The Blues trailed 2-0 until the 12:22 mark of the third period before scoring three unanswered goals.
Joel Hofer was spectacular in net for St. Louis, making 36 saves to earn his 8th win in 12 starts.
Connor McDavid scored his 37th goal of the season, while Leon Draisaitl extended his point streak to eight games.
Oilers goaltender Connor Ingram recorded a rare goalie assist on McDavid's third-period goal.
games.context: The Blues' sixth win in seven games pulls them within five points of a Western Conference wild-card spot.

highlights.title: Oilers vs. Blues | NHL Highlights | March 13, 2026
